reasoning: The 5-year Treasury yield ended 2025 at 3.74 having been stable / slightly increasing since late September (around 3.65 to 3.74) despite an overall year-long downward move. The statistical analysis from FRED based on recent trends and seasonality produces a combined probability of 0.60 for a near-term increase above 3.74. With short-term upward momentum but the longer-term backdrop of an ongoing Fed easing cycle and easing inflation, yields are likely to face some downward pressure over the medium term. Therefore, I place the highest probability of increase in the very short term (0.58 at week 1) where recent positive momentum dominates. As the forecast horizon extends to 1-month and 3-month dates, uncertainty rises and the likelihood of the broader downward macro trend asserting itself brings my probability closer to the 0.5 neutral mark, resulting in 0.54 and 0.51 respectively.
